\documentclass{amsart}
\usepackage{amsmath,amsthm}

\newtheorem{theorem}{Theorem}[section]
\newtheorem{lemma}[theorem]{Lemma}

\begin{document}

\section{Warmup}
\label{sec:warmup}

\subsection{Nested statements}
\label{subsec:nested}

\begin{lemma}\label{lem:outer}
The identities \eqref{eq:nested-align} and \eqref{eq:inline} live inside this statement.
\begin{align}\label{eq:nested-align}
a &= b
\end{align}
\begin{eqnarray}\label{eq:inline} c &=& d \end{eqnarray}
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
By Lemma \ref{lem:inner} and \eqref{eq:proof}, we finish.
\begin{lemma}\label{lem:inner}
Nested lemma inside a proof.
\end{lemma}
\begin{equation}\label{eq:proof}
1 = 1
\end{equation}
\end{proof}

\subsubsection{Detailed case}
\label{subsubsec:detail}

\begin{theorem}\label{thm:uses-headings}
From \ref{subsec:nested}, \ref{subsubsec:detail}, and \eqref{eq:nested-align}, the claim follows.
\end{theorem}

\end{document}
